carbon_policy_signal.item: EU ETS Commission review scheduled July 2026; Poland political pressure on cap and allowance rules ahead of formal review; potential trigger for free-allowance expansion or cap-loosening.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: EUA forward curve at risk if July review signals cap expansion or free-allowance loosening; could re-price carbon-cost embedded in power merit order and dark spreads into H2 2026.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (160 days old, as of 2025-12-26); clean-dark stale (155 days old, as of 2025-12-31). Coal 7th-pctile and clean-dark 49th-pctile merit order ranks may be outdated; assume merit-order repricing risk if gas/storage dynamics shift.
risk_flags: GB Power -57% daily, 7th-percentile: renewable saturation risk persists; grid curtailment/negative pricing tail-risk if wind persistence continues.
EU Storage 41% (14 pp below 5-yr seasonal): June refill pace critical; any shortfall into summer tightens Aug/Sept baseload and lifts Cal+1 power.
ETS July 2026 review: Poland political pressure on cap/allowances could re-price EUA forward curve (currently 39th-pctile, 33.30 EUR/t); watch volatility into June.
Coal data stale (160 days old, 7th-pctile at $96/t); clean-dark at 49th-pctile (27.95 EUR/MWh) — merit order may be shifting off snapshot; coal out-of-merit risks underestimated.

base.summary: Renewable-led suppression of GB/DE DA continues; EU storage refill steady; TTF stable mid-60s; EUA holds 33–34 EUR/t ahead of July review.

downside.summary: US LNG capex ramp increases global supply; Henry Hub soft; TTF arbitrage widens; renewable wind/solar persistence sustains DA suppression; AI demand flattening extends.

upside.summary: El Niño heat wave drives summer cooling demand; Alpine/Nordic hydro shortfall tightens intraday baseload; storage refill falters into July.

themes: GB power collapse amid renewable glut
EU storage deficit pressuring summer gas/power arbitrage
Coal historically suppressed; dark spread neutral
Policy tailwinds (green fiscal exemption, ETS review risk)
top_drivers: GB Power 7th-pctile collapse: 53% renewable share (74th-pctile) saturating grid; curtailment/negative pricing risk if wind/solar persistence; demand flattening via AI load-shift compresses peak margins.
EU Storage 14 pp seasonal deficit (17th-pctile, 41% full): refill pace through June critical for summer baseload arbitrage; Aug/Sept tightness will lift Cal+1 power if builds miss seasonal.
Coal at 7th-pctile ($96/t), clean-dark neutral (49th-pctile); merit order out-of-dispatch — watch for fast repricing if gas rises or storage tightens, pulling coal back into marginal stack.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 7th-percentile (48.23 EUR/MWh) amid 53% renewable share; EU storage 14 pp below seasonal drives summer margin risk; coal/dark spreads historically compressed.

watchlist.why: 53.18% of load (74th-pctile); demand flattening via AI load-shift reduces peak-hour price support; monitor for further curtailment/negative pricing.
41% full, 17th-pctile; summer tightness into Aug/Sept will drive baseload and forward premium; watch weekly builds vs 5-yr seasonal path.
July 2026 Commission ETS review flagged by Poland; any signaling of cap-loosening or free-allowance expansion could re-price carbon-cost embedded in power spreads.
US pipeline capex (45 Bcf/d 2026–27) will increase LNG export supply; monitor Henry Hub and spot LNG prices for downward TTF arbitrage pressure.
Global heat/extreme weather forecast; summer cooling demand and Alpine/Nordic hydro tightness could support intraday/peak baseload.

watchlist_dated.name: EU–Trump trade vote (Jun 16)
watchlist_dated.why: Tariff escalation risk feeds transatlantic cost inflation → power input costs

watchlist_dated.name: EU ETS review (Jul 2026)
watchlist_dated.why: Political consensus on cap/MSR/allowances critical for Q3–Q4 curve re-pricing
